A Jupiter aspect on your 7th house can bless relationships; Saturn aspecting the 7th may delay or deepen commitment. Always read the planet's nature first.
A complete read combines planet, sign, house, and aspect.
A planet in House 2 aspects House 8 through its 7th aspect.
A sign is 30°; a nakshatra is 13°20'.
A solid beginner read uses Lagna, Moon, Kendras, aspects, and conjunctions — always with accurate birth time.
Adopts the nature of planets near it

All planets aspect the 7th; Mars, Jupiter, and Saturn have additional special aspects.

Any planet sitting in your 1st house paints your personality strongly. A 1st-house Mars looks athletic and bold; Venus brings charm and beauty.
Arrange Kendra houses in numerical order (lowest to highest).
Arrange the three fire signs in zodiac order (first → last in the year cycle).
Aspects can be ignored in a superficial chart read.
Avoid these pitfalls — especially around aspects.
Beginners often hear "benefic" and "malefic." Think of benefics as gentle teachers; malefics as strict coaches. Context always matters — a "hard" planet in the right place can build strength.
Benefic aspect: growth, teachers, optimism in those houses
Bhavas — the stages of your life story
Birth time and place matter — Lagna changes every ~2 hours.
Build the foundation first (Lagna, Moon, houses), then layer in Drishti and conjunctions.
Cardinal (Chara): starts things — Mesha, Karka, Tula, Makara
Chandra (Moon) is the karaka of the mind — your feelings, moods, and instinctive reactions.
Chandra is the karaka (significator) of the mind.
Conjunction means two planets share the same house or sign.
Connect each element to one of its Vedic fire/earth/air/water sign examples.
Connect each house number to its main theme.
Don't rush predictions — observe patterns for weeks. The chart reveals tendencies, not fixed fate.
Drishti means "gaze" or "aspect." A planet not only affects the house it sits in — it also influences other houses it "looks at." Think of each Graha casting a beam of its energy across the chart.
Drishti shows hidden influences on Lagna, 7th, and 10th.
Each Nakshatra has 4 Padas (quarters) of 3°20' each. Pada links Nakshatra to the navamsa (D9) chart — advanced topic, but you'll see "pada" on detailed charts.
Each Nakshatra has a deity, symbol, and temperament. You don't need to memorize all 27 now — start with your own Moon Nakshatra on your chart.
Each Nakshatra is smaller than a full Rashi (sign).
Earth rotates once every 24 hours. The zodiac appears to rise at about 1 sign every 2 hours. Twins born 90 minutes apart can have different Lagnas.
Effect: activates, heats up, or creates pressure in aspected houses
Even beginners benefit from noting Jupiter/Saturn aspects on important houses.
Every planet aspects the 7th house from its own position. This is the opposition — a direct, full-strength gaze across the chart wheel.
Every sign belongs to Fire, Earth, Air, or Water. Elements describe temperament — how energy moves through you.
Fixed (Sthira): holds steady — Vrishabha, Simha, Vrishchika, Kumbha
Flip each card to memorize the core meaning. View all cards to continue.
Forgetting that all planets aspect the 7th house
Full integration — planets, houses, aspects & more
Generally challenging: Saturn, Mars, Rahu, Ketu, Sun (can burn)
Generally gentle: Jupiter, Venus, waxing Moon, well-placed Mercury
Grahas — cosmic forces that color your life
Guru brings growth, wisdom, and protection when well placed.
Houses 1, 4, 7, and 10 are Kendras (angles). They are the strongest, most visible parts of life.
Houses 1, 5, and 9 are Trikonas (trines) — dharma, creativity, and fortune. They support growth and protection.
Houses are 12 life departments. House 1 starts at Lagna; houses 2–12 follow counter-clockwise. Planets in a house activate that life area.
How many Nakshatras divide the Vedic zodiac?

How to Count Aspects on Your Chart

If Jupiter sits in House 1, its 7th aspect falls on House 7 — partnerships get Jupiter's protection and growth. If Jupiter is in House 5, its 7th aspect still hits House 11, AND its special 9th aspect reaches House 1 (Lagna). Trace each planet carefully.
Ignoring birth time → wrong Lagna and wrong aspects
In Vedic astrology, Moon is often more important than Sun sign.
In the 7-step formula, when do you check Drishti (aspects)?
Jupiter (Guru) aspects the 5th, 7th, and 9th houses from its position. Jupiter's gaze expands, protects, and brings wisdom — often the most helpful aspects in a chart.
Jupiter in House 1 → aspects Houses 5, 7, and 9
Jupiter in the 10th often brings career wisdom; Moon in the 4th deepens home life. Always read planet + sign + house together.
Jupiter is generally considered the most benefic planet.
Jyotish (ज्योतिष) means "science of light." For thousands of years, Indian astrologers have used the sky as a mirror of karma — not fortune-telling, but a map of tendencies you can work with.
Kanya is the Vedic name for Libra.
Karka ♋, Vrishchika ♏, Meena ♓ — emotional, intuitive, and deeply feeling.
Lagna (लग्न) means "ascendant" — the zodiac degree rising on the eastern horizon at your birth. It sets the entire house system of your chart.
Lagna is a sign (30°); Nakshatra is a finer 13°20' lunar mansion.
Lagna is the same as your Nakshatra.

Learn the Sanskrit Vedic names. Flip each card and view all to continue.
Leo's ruler is Surya — royal, fiery energy.
Makara = Earth, Kumbha = Air, Meena = Water.
Makara, Kumbha, and Meena are all fire signs.
Mars (Mangal) has extra Drishti on the 4th, 7th, and 8th houses from where it sits. Mars brings energy, conflict, or courage to those life areas.
Mars + Saturn: disciplined action, frustration if blocked
Mars aspects 4th, 7th, 8th from its house — from 10 that's 1, 4, 5.
Mars casts intense energy to the 4th, 7th, and 8th houses — plus it has the universal 7th aspect like all Grahas.
Mars in House 1 → aspects Houses 4, 7, and 8
Mars in House 10 aspects House 1 through its 4th aspect.
Mars specially aspects which houses (from its position)?

Mesha Lagna looks bold and direct. Mars (chart ruler) in the 10th house fuels career ambition. Mars also aspects the 1st (4th aspect from 10), 4th, and 5th houses — energizing home and creativity too.
Mesha ♈, Simha ♌, Dhanu ♐ — passionate, direct, and future-oriented.
Mithuna ♊, Tula ♎, Kumbha ♒ — mental, social, and idea-driven.
Modalities show how signs initiate, sustain, or adapt. This helps you compare signs beyond elements alone.
Moon + Venus: charm, love of comfort
Moon in Karka needs emotional safety. If Saturn in House 4 aspects Moon's house (via 7th or special aspect), emotions may feel heavy or delayed — but Saturn also builds deep resilience over time.
Moon is the mind; many Vedic techniques center on it.
Moon nakshatra deeply colors mental and emotional patterns.
Moon sign = how you feel and react inside
Moon's Nakshatra is especially important; all planets occupy a nakshatra.
Most benefic planet in Vedic astrology
Mutable (Dvisvabhava): adapts — Mithuna, Kanya, Dhanu, Meena
Nakshatras are only used for the Sun, not the Moon.
Nine celestial bodies — the Grahas — "grasp" or influence your life. Seven are physical (Sun through Saturn), plus Rahu and Ketu, the shadow nodes of the Moon.
Not "bad" — Saturn aspects build long-term strength
On our Dashboard chart, look for House 1 or "Asc." The sign in that house is your Lagna. In South Indian charts, it is often marked clearly at the top diamond.
On the Dashboard chart, trace lines mentally from each planet — start with Jupiter and Saturn as they affect wide areas.
On your birth chart, find the Moon symbol (☽). The sign AND nakshatra name will be listed — e.g. "Moon in Rohini." Read a simple description online to reflect on your emotional style.
Only Mars and Jupiter have aspects in Vedic astrology.

Open your chart. Run the 7 steps on paper: write Lagna, Moon, Sun, Kendra planets, Jupiter/Saturn positions, then list every Drishti onto Houses 1, 7, and 10. Circle any conjunctions. This 15-minute ritual is your first real reading.
Order Jupiter's three special aspect house numbers from lowest to highest.

The 3rd house is courage, siblings, and skills you build. The 11th is friends, income, and wishes fulfilled.
The 7th house is the house of the other — spouse and open partnerships.
The Ascendant moves quickly — roughly one sign every two hours.
The Moon represents the mind and emotions in Vedic astrology.
The commander. Mars is energy, courage, siblings, land, surgery, and the will to fight for what you want.
The great teacher. Jupiter expands wisdom, children, wealth, dharma (right path), and optimism.
The king of the chart. Surya is your atma (soul), vitality, father, authority, and confidence in the world.
The minister of pleasure. Venus governs love, marriage, art, luxury, vehicles, and harmony.
The prince of communication. Mercury rules speech, writing, trade, humor, and analytical thinking.
The queen of the chart. Chandra is your manas (mind), emotions, mother, comfort, and how you nurture yourself.
The slow elder. Saturn teaches patience through delay, discipline, labor, and karma from past efforts.
The whole house wheel is anchored to the Ascendant.
The zodiac is a 360° belt divided into 12 equal Rashis of 30° each. Planets travel through these signs; the sign shows the style or flavor of a planet's energy.
The zodiac is also divided into 27 Nakshatras of 13°20' each. They come from the Moon's daily motion and ancient star lore. Your Moon Nakshatra is deeply personal in Vedic astrology.
Their energies blend in that life area.
There are 27 Nakshatras in the Vedic zodiac system.
These are not physical planets but mathematical points where Moon's path crosses the Sun's. They create eclipses and karmic hunger or release.
These houses often bring effort or transformation. Beginners should not fear them — they show where life asks you to grow.
They are lunar nodes — shadow points, not physical bodies.
This course gives you a beginner's lens — enough to read your own chart superficially.
Treating Saturn aspects as only negative
Two people born on the same calendar day always share the same Lagna.
Using only Sun sign like Western horoscopes
Vedic and Western astrology use the same zodiac symbols (♈♉♊...).
Vedic aspects are house-based (from the whole-sign house a planet occupies), not degree-exact like some Western techniques.
Vedic astrology is Chandra-oriented. The Moon shows the mind (manas), comfort, and emotional habits. Many predictive techniques start from Moon position, not Sun.
Vrishabha ♉, Kanya ♍, Makara ♑ — grounded, practical, and results-focused.
Waxing Moon is considered gentler than waning
Weak Sun: low confidence, strained father themes
Western pop astrology emphasizes the Sun sign. Vedic astrology prioritizes Lagna, then Moon, then Sun — like outer self, inner mind, and soul purpose.
When two or more planets sit in the same sign/house, they are in conjunction. Their energies blend — sometimes harmoniously, sometimes like roommates who argue.
